content: IMU sensor pipeline architecture: Phone captures DeviceOrientationEvent (alpha/beta/gamma in degrees) at OS rate ~60-100Hz. DeviceMotionEvent provides linear_acceleration (g-corrected) and rotation_rate. Madgwick filter runs on-phone only — it fuses gyro+accel+mag into a stable quaternion, compensating for gyro drift using acc/mag gravity reference. Output: quaternion q [w,x,y,z]. ZUPT detection: when |linear_acceleration| < threshold for N consecutive samples, declare stationary, reset velocity to zero in Kalman state. Kalman filter maintains [pos, vel] state: predict step integrates accel (double-integrate), update step applies ZUPT or other observations. Dead reckoning position is gesture-window relative, not absolute. Pipeline stages: (1) Raw sensor read → (2) Madgwick update → (3) Kalman predict → (4) ZUPT detect → (5) Kalman update → (6) Pack to binary → (7) RTCDataChannel.send(ArrayBuffer). Desktop side: RTCDataChannel.onmessage → (1) Unpack binary → (2) seq/timestamp check → (3) Apply quaternion to THREE.Object3D.quaternion.copy(q) or .slerp(q, factor).
